examples.title: Algorithm: Servlet Lifecycle Process
examples.description: Demonstrates the sequence of methods called during a servlet's lifecycle
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ServletLifecycle
BEGIN
 INITIALIZE servlet with config parameters
 HANDLE incoming requests through service method
 ROUTE to appropriate doGet/doPost based on HTTP method
 DESTROY servlet when container shuts down
END
examples.explanation:
1. INITIALIZE: init() method called once during servlet initialization
2. HANDLE: service() method routes requests to doGet/doPost
3. DESTROY: destroy() called during servlet termination

examples.title: Basic Servlet with Lifecycle Methods
examples.description: Implementation of init(), doGet(), and destroy() methods in a servlet
examples.explanation:
1. init(): Called once during servlet initialization
2. doGet(): Handles GET requests
3. destroy(): Called when servlet is taken out of service

examples.title: Handling POST Request with Parameters
examples.description: Using doPost() method to process form data
examples.explanation:
1. Retrieves parameters using request.getParameter()
2. Processes and outputs received data
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is input size

examples.title: Setting Cookies in a Servlet
examples.description: Creating and adding cookies to the HTTP response
examples.explanation:
1. Creates Cookie object with name-value pair
2. Sets max age for persistence
3. Adds cookie to response using addCookie()

examples.title: JSP Lifecycle Methods Implementation
examples.description: Overriding jspInit() and jspDestroy() in a JSP page
examples.code:
<%@ page language="java" contentType="text/html; charset=UTF-8"%> <%! public void jspInit() { System.out.println("JSP Initialized"); } public void jspDestroy() { System.out.println("JSP Destroyed"); } %>

JSP Lifecycle Demo
examples.explanation:
1. jspInit(): Called during JSP initialization
2. jspDestroy(): Called when JSP is removed
3. _jspService() is auto-generated
